name: 深圳
description: Shenzhen is China's first Special Economic Zone and a global tech hub. A modern megacity that transformed from a fishing village to one of the world's fastest-growing cities in just 40 years. Home to tech giants like Tencent, Huawei, and BYD. Features innovative architecture, theme parks, and easy access to Hong Kong.
highlights: Window of the World - Global landmarks

climate.tips: Best visited in winter (Nov-Feb) when weather is mild and pleasant. Summer is hot, humid with frequent typhoons. Spring is rainy. Always bring sunscreen and hydration.

attractions.name: 世界之窗
attractions.description: Theme park featuring miniature replicas of famous world landmarks including Eiffel Tower, pyramids, and Taj Mahal.
attractions.tips: Start early to cover all attractions. Evening is best for photos. Book tickets online for discount.

attractions.name: 华侨城创意园
attractions.description: Former industrial complex converted into vibrant arts and design hub with galleries, boutiques, cafes, and creative studios.
attractions.address: North Area of OCT, Nanshan District
attractions.tips: Best on weekends for markets and events. The area transforms during Chinese New Year with art exhibitions.

attractions.name: 大芬油画村
attractions.description: The world's largest oil painting production base, where millions of replicas of famous artworks are painted. Now also an art destination.
attractions.tips: Bargaining expected when buying paintings. Quality varies widely. Commission paintings on-site at reasonable prices.

attractions.name: 深圳湾公园
attractions.description: Coastal park along Shenzhen Bay with scenic waterfront, mangroves, bird watching, and Hong Kong skyline views.
attractions.tips: Best at sunset for Hong Kong skyline photos. Morning for bird watching. Bring binoculars.

attractions.name: 海上世界
attractions.description: Italian-themed waterfront complex centered on a historic Italian cruise ship, featuring restaurants, bars, and entertainment.
attractions.tips: Evening visit recommended for atmosphere. Great for seafood restaurants. Take ferry from Hong Kong for scenic arrival.

attractions.name: 欢乐谷
attractions.description: Large amusement park with roller coasters, water rides, and performances for all ages.
attractions.tips: Weekday visits avoid crowds. Height restrictions on rides. Re-entry allowed with hand stamp.

attractions.name: 平安金融中心
attractions.description: The second tallest building in China with observation deck offering panoramic city views from 542 meters.
attractions.tips: Book tickets online to avoid queues. Go at sunset for day and night views. Avoid foggy days.

attractions.name: 海岸城
attractions.description: Large waterfront shopping complex with international brands, restaurants, and entertainment in Nanshan.
attractions.tips: Good for rainy days. Connected to Metro. Excellent restaurants on upper floors.

attractions.name: 东部华侨城
attractions.description: Large ecological tourism zone with茶溪谷 theme park,大侠谷云海谷, and hot springs. Combines natural scenery with entertainment.
attractions.tips: Stay overnight at the resort hotel for full experience. Wear comfortable shoes for hiking.

attractions.name: 大梅沙海滨公园
attractions.description: Popular sandy beach with clear waters and scenic coastline. Free entry public beach with facilities.
attractions.tips: Arrive early on weekends to avoid crowds. No outside food allowed. Rent umbrella and chair on-site.

attractions.name: 小梅沙海洋世界
attractions.description: Marine aquarium with dolphin shows, polar exhibits, and underwater tunnels. Great for families.
attractions.tips: Shows at scheduled times, check schedule on arrival. Good for rainy days.

attractions.name: 荷兰花卉小镇
attractions.description: European-style flower market with Dutch architecture, flower shops, and cafes. Free entry.
attractions.tips: Best in spring for flower displays. Popular for wedding photos. Great for Instagram shots.

attractions.name: 甘坑古镇
attractions.description: Historical Hakka village with traditional architecture, museums, and local snacks. Evening lighting spectacular.
attractions.tips: Visit in late afternoon to see both day and night scenes. Try Hakka specialties.

attractions.name: 观澜版画村
attractions.description: Traditional Hakka village turned artist colony. Known for printmaking and traditional buildings.
attractions.tips: Combine with nearby Guanlan Hot Spring. Morning visits best for photos.

attractions.name: 大鹏所城
attractions.description: 600-year-old Ming Dynasty coastal fortress. Birthplace of China's modern navy and Hakka culture.
attractions.tips: Combine with nearby beaches. Try Dapeng cuisine. Half-day trip recommended.

attractions.name: 较场尾海滩
attractions.description: Popular beach destination known for surfing, beach bars, and colorful seaside cabins.
attractions.tips: Known as 'Shenzhen's Bali'. Stay overnight for beach party atmosphere. Try fresh seafood.

attractions.name: 观澜湖高尔夫
attractions.description: World's largest golf club with 12 courses. Hosts international tournaments.
attractions.tips: Book in advance. Non-golfers can enjoy spa and restaurants. Stay at resort hotel.

attractions.name: 深圳博物馆
attractions.description: Modern museum showcasing Shenzhen's history from fishing village to megacity. Free entry.
attractions.tips: Combine with nearby Shenzhen Convention Center. Good for rainy days.

attractions.name: 春 节
attractions.description: Major citywide celebrations during Chinese New Year with flowers, lights, and cultural events.
attractions.tips: Book hotels early. City empties after 3rd day. Good time to visit theme parks without crowds.

attractions.name: 大梅沙海滨公园
attractions.description: Popular coastal park with sandy beach and recreational facilities.
attractions.tips: Best on weekdays to avoid crowds. Take sun protection.

restaurants.name: 利苑酒家
restaurants.address: 2F, Shenzhen Times Square, Luobao Road, Luohu
restaurants.description: Two Michelin-starred Cantonese fine dining with exceptional dim sum and premium seafood.

restaurants.name: 粤菜王府
restaurants.address: 2F, Park 'N Shop Center, Jintian Road, Futian
restaurants.description: Two-diamond Black Pearl restaurant specializing in authentic Cantonese cuisine with elegant setting.

restaurants.name: 润园四季椰子鸡
restaurants.nameEn: Run Yuan Si Ji Coconut Chicken
restaurants.description: Famous for tender coconut chicken hot pot, a favorite among locals for healthy and delicious dining.

restaurants.name: 金稻园砂锅粥
restaurants.description: Popular late-night spot for thick Cantonese congee and fresh seafood dishes.

restaurants.name: 华强北小吃街
restaurants.description: Bustling street food area near electronics market with variety of local snacks and quick bites.

restaurants.name: 蔡澜点心
restaurants.description: Michelin-starred dim sum by celebrity chef Chua Lam. Modern presentation with traditional flavors.

restaurants.name: 日日香鹅肉馆
restaurants.description: Famous for salt-baked goose and traditional Chaozhou dishes. Local favorite with honest pricing.

restaurants.name: 探鱼
restaurants.description: Popular chain known for creatively grilled fish dishes with Sichuan spices and local flavors.

restaurants.name: 点都德
restaurants.address: Various locations including Dongmen and Futian
restaurants.description: Popular dim sum chain known for traditional recipes and all-day dining. Great for breakfast and brunch.

restaurants.name: 陶陶居
restaurants.description: Historic Cantonese restaurant established in 1880. Known for authentic old-style dim sum and traditional dishes.

restaurants.name: 广州酒家
restaurants.description: Famous chain for Cantonese cuisine and yum cha. Known for moon cakes and holiday specialties.

restaurants.name: 胜记酒家
restaurants.description: Legendary Cantonese restaurant with 40+ years history. Known for premium seafood and traditional recipes.

restaurants.name: 潮江春
restaurants.description: Elegant Chaozhou restaurant known for精致潮州菜 and superior service.

restaurants.name: 丹桂轩
restaurants.description: Popular for all-day dim sum and Cantonese comfort food. Local favorite with consistent quality.

restaurants.name: 海港大酒楼
restaurants.description: Large traditional Cantonese restaurant famous for wedding banquets and yum cha.

restaurants.name: 凤凰楼
restaurants.description: Classic dim sum restaurant in historic Dongmen area. Known for affordable and authentic yum cha.

restaurants.name: 大名酒家
restaurants.description: Neighborhood favorite for traditional Cantonese breakfast and dim sum. Local crowd only.

restaurants.name: 老广酒家
restaurants.description: Known for authentic old Guangzhou style dim sum. Opens early for breakfast lovers.

restaurants.name: 波士顿龙虾餐厅
restaurants.description: Premium seafood restaurant famous for live tanks with lobster, crab, and fish.

restaurants.name: BBQ韩国烤肉
restaurants.description: Popular Korean BBQ with premium meat selection and authentic Korean side dishes.

arrival.duration: Varies by origin (3h from Hong Kong, 12h from Europe)
arrival.frequency: Daily flights from Asia, Europe, Americas
arrival.tips: Airport is 35km from city center. Metro Line 11 to downtown. Airport shuttle bus available.

arrival.to: Shenzhen Railway Station / Shenzhen North Station / Shenzhen East
arrival.tips: High-speed rail from Hong Kong West Kowloon is very convenient. Futian Station is central.

bus: Extensive network covering areas not served by metro
Many routes run until late night
taxi: Didi works well for English users
bike: Designated bike lanes on many roads
Shared bikes forbidden on some roads

hotels.name: 深圳华侨城洲际酒店
hotels.bookingTips: Weekend packages good value. Check for OCT discounts.

hotels.name: 深圳瑞吉酒店
hotels.bookingTips: Club rooms worth upgrade for privileges. Book direct for benefits.

hotels.name: 深圳香格里拉大酒店
hotels.bookingTips: Best for those entering from Hong Kong. Good for transit.

hotels.name: 深圳格兰云天酒店
hotels.bookingTips: Check weekend rates. Book early during conventions.

hotels.name: 深圳东门酒店
hotels.bookingTips: Great for shopping tourists. Basic but clean.

hotels.name: 深圳jw万豪酒店
hotels.bookingTips: Book via Marriott app for member benefits. Club lounge access worth it.

hotels.name: 深圳大中华希尔顿酒店
hotels.bookingTips: Higher floors offer better views. Weekend deals available.

hotels.name: 深圳威尼斯睿途酒店
hotels.bookingTips: Request canal-view rooms. Good for families with kids.

hotels.name: 深圳求山水皇冠假日酒店
hotels.bookingTips: Book during conventions in Futian for convenience.

hotels.name: 深圳海德酒店
hotels.bookingTips: Good for tech company visitors. Check corporate rates.

hotels.name: 深圳艺尚商务酒店
hotels.bookingTips: Best value in Futian. Good for short business trips.

hotels.name: 深圳凯宾斯基酒店
hotels.bookingTips: Sea-view rooms worth premium. Book direct for better rates.

hotels.name: 深圳马哥孛罗酒店
hotels.bookingTips: Great for cross-border travelers. Connected to mall.

hotels.name: 深圳深业上城民宿
hotels.bookingTips: Good for extended stays. Request higher floors.

hotels.name: 深圳北站铂尔曼酒店
hotels.bookingTips: Perfect for arriving by train. Easy metro connection.

hotels.name: 深圳雅诗阁服务公寓
hotels.bookingTips: Best for business travelers staying a week or more.

hotels.name: 深圳中洲圣廷苑酒店
hotels.bookingTips: Good balance of price and location. Weekend discounts.

hotels.name: 深圳东部华侨城茵特拉根酒店
hotels.bookingTips: Include hot spring in your booking. Great for weekend getaways.

culturalTips.title: Tech Culture
culturalTips.content: Shenzhen is China's tech hub. Visit Huaqiangbei electronics market for gadgets. Experience cashless society firsthand.

culturalTips.title: Fast Pace
culturalTips.content: Shenzhen is always moving. Metro is packed during rush hours (7:30-9:00, 17:30-19:30). Plan accordingly.

culturalTips.title: Dining Hours
culturalTips.content: Local restaurants busy during normal meal times: 11:30-13:30 and 17:30-20:30. Late-night dining common.

culturalTips.title: Language
culturalTips.content: Mandarin is primary. Cantonese widely spoken in Luohu and traditional areas. English more common in tourist areas.

culturalTips.title: Huaqiangbei Electronics Market
culturalTips.content: The world's largest electronics market at Huaqiangbei. Bargain hard, check quality carefully, and verify specifications before purchase. Bring your laptop to test components.

culturalTips.title: Startup Ecosystem
culturalTips.content: Shenzhen is home to Tencent, Huawei, BYD, and DJI. Visit Tencent's headquarters in Nanshan. The city has a unique entrepreneurial spirit born from reform and opening.

culturalTips.title: Cashless Society
culturalTips.content: Mobile payment is universal in Shenzhen. Alipay and WeChat Pay are used for everything from street food to taxis. Bring a phone with these apps installed.

culturalTips.title: Beach Culture
culturalTips.content: Shenzhen has beautiful beaches (Dameisha, Xiaomeisha, Jiaochangwei). Arrive early on weekends as they get crowded. Bring sunscreen - shade is limited.

culturalTips.title: Cantonese Influence
culturalTips.content: While Mandarin dominates, Cantonese is still widely spoken in Luohu, Dongmen, and traditional Hakka areas. Locals appreciate when visitors try basic phrases.

culturalTips.title: Hong Kong Access
culturalTips.content: Shenzhen is gateway to Hong Kong via Luohu or Futian Checkpoint. Consider a day trip to Hong Kong or use Shenzhen as base for Pearl River Delta exploration.

culturalTips.title: Theme Park Culture
culturalTips.content: Shenzhen has major theme parks (Window of the World, Happy Valley, OCT Eastern). Book tickets online for discounts. Weekdays are less crowded.

culturalTips.title: Fashion and Design
culturalTips.content: OCT-LOFT and Dapeng Fortress host creative festivals. Shenzhen Fashion Week showcases local designers. The city blends innovation with manufacturing.

culturalTips.title: Reform History
culturalTips.content: Shenzhen was a fishing village in 1980. Now it's 17+ million people. Visit Shenzhen Museum to understand this remarkable transformation.

culturalTips.title: Teochew Dialect
culturalTips.content: Shenzhen has large Teochew (潮州) population from Chaoshan. Their cuisine and culture influence local dining. Try Chaoshan beef hot pot.

culturalTips.title: Seafood Dining
culturalTips.content: Coastal location means fresh seafood is abundant. Shekou and Dapeng have excellent seafood restaurants. Live tanks are common - pick your own.

culturalTips.title: Nightlife Scene
culturalTips.content: Sea World (海上世界) in Shekou has vibrant nightlife with international bars. Coastal City and COCO Park also have good evening options.

culturalTips.title: Art Scene
culturalTips.content: Dafen Oil Painting Village creates 60% of world's oil paintings. OCT-LOFT hosts indie music and design events. Art is big business here.

culturalTips.title: Typhoon Season
culturalTips.content: Shenzhen typhoon season is July-September. Check weather before beach trips. Major typhoons cause transit shutdowns - stay indoors.

culturalTips.title: Modern Architecture
culturalTips.content: Shenzhen has iconic modern architecture: Ping An Finance Center (599m), Shenzhen Civic Center by Mario Botta. Architecture tours are popular.

culturalTips.title: Business Hub
culturalTips.content: Shenzhen is China's Silicon Valley. Many tech companies recruit internationally. Business culture is fast-paced and efficiency-focused.

quickFacts.visa: Most nationalities require visa; Hong Kong visit if eligible
quickFacts.water: Tap water safe; bottled water widely available
